01 · Profile

I design at the intersection of finance, data, and visual storytelling, making complex ideas look exactly as significant as they are.

As Technical Head of the Finance & Investment Cell, Kirori Mal College (DU), I built the official FIC website on Framer, drove an Instagram content strategy that reached 650,000+ accounts in 30 days generating 1.5M+ impressions, and coordinated 4,000+ event registrations. Currently designing YouTube thumbnails as a Graphic Design Intern at The Study Anchor, contributing to 15,000+ channel views.

My work spans editorial graphics, event campaigns, speaker series, and brand design. The common thread: every piece has a point of view. I don't decorate, I communicate.

03 · Selected Work

Projects that
did the job

Finance & Investment Cell, Kirori Mal College · 2024–2026

01

Editorial · News Graphic

Inside NSE: Where Milliseconds Meant Millions

An editorial thumbnail for FIC's deep-dive into the NSE co-location scandal, one of India's most significant stock exchange controversies. The brief demanded gravitas without sensationalism.

Executed in a dark investigative palette, crimson, near-black, and high-contrast type, to mirror the weight of the story. The composition guides the eye from the institution to the face to the charge.

Canva Photoshop

02

Campaign · Corporate Analysis

Two Giants, One Vision: Reinventing Insurance

Visual coverage of the Jio–Allianz joint venture, a landmark insurance deal that rewrote Indian market assumptions. Had to convey corporate scale while keeping the story accessible to a student audience.

The purple grid backdrop signals institutional authority. Typography does the heavy lifting, bold claim at top, editorial whitespace in between, image trio anchoring the bottom.

Canva Photoshop

03

Event Campaign · Editorial Branding

FraudFlix, Adhimulya Editorial

A visual explainer for FIC's Union Budget coverage, designed to make a dense policy story feel immediate, polished, and easy to scan.

Borrowed Breaking Bad's visual language, a lone figure, green-acid light, chaotic data environment, to signal both high stakes and narrative intrigue. The competition structure (quiz → submission → presentation) is integrated without cluttering the hero visual.

Canva Photoshop

04

Editorial · Geopolitical Commentary

Tariffs Today, Trouble Tomorrow?

A visual essay starter on India–US trade tensions, covering the Trump-era tariff narrative for FIC's content series. Required balancing political weight with accessibility.

Two figures facing inward across a container-port backdrop creates instant compositional tension. The desaturated subjects against a vivid magenta field was a deliberate choice to universalize the narrative beyond the individual actors.

Canva Photoshop

05

Brand Design · Automotive

Porsche 911 GT3, Editorial Brand Poster

A spec-sheet transformed into a brand moment. The challenge: make technical data feel like poetry, in the visual language of a company that's spent 25 years perfecting its story.

Large letterform behind the car creates depth without clutter. Minimal color palette (black, white, the car) lets the specs speak. Every element justifies its position.

Canva Photoshop

06

Identity Design · Team Branding

Core Team Identity Cards, FIC 2025–26

Annual team introduction series for FIC's incoming core committee. Each card had to communicate personality, role, and the organization's brand, simultaneously, in a single square frame.

The ID card motif grounds the design in professional context while annotations around the card add a personal, editorial dimension. A repeatable system scaled across 10+ team members while keeping each card distinct.

Canva

07

Event Design · Speaker Series

MULYA Speaker Session, Shri V. Vualnam

Official speaker session poster for FIC's Mulya initiative, featuring Shri V. Vualnam, Expenditure Secretary of India and Former Secretary, Ministry of Civil Aviation. A high-stakes design brief requiring institutional credibility alongside visual energy.

The dark diagonal texture creates kinetic energy behind a composed professional portrait. Typographic hierarchy resolves the tension between event excitement and speaker gravitas, the name and title land with authority at the bottom, anchoring the composition.

Canva Photoshop
